## Hotfix: agent registration never succeeded in v0.3.0

`sentinel-polkit-agent` v0.3.0 used `logind.GetSessionByPID()` to
resolve the agent's session id for `RegisterAuthenticationAgent`. That
D-Bus method requires polkit authorization which an unprivileged
session-scoped agent doesn't have, so registration always failed with:

```
Error: Authority.RegisterAuthenticationAgent
Caused by:
    org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.AccessDenied: Sender is not authorized
```

Fix: read `/proc/self/sessionid` (kernel audit subsystem; set by
`audit_setloginuid` at login, inherited through forks) instead, with
`XDG_SESSION_ID` and `--session-id` as fallbacks. Same source other
polkit agents use; no D-Bus call required.

## What you'll see post-upgrade on cosmic-comp

After log out / log back in, the agent now successfully detects its
session and *attempts* registration. On cosmic-comp specifically you
may see:

```
Error: An authentication agent already exists for the given subject
```

That's a different problem: `cosmic-session` spawns `cosmic-osd` as a
supervised child and re-spawns it when it dies. Both agents race for
polkit's registered-agent slot, and cosmic-osd usually wins because
cosmic-session starts it first and keeps it alive.

To make Sentinel the active agent on cosmic-comp, you currently have
to evict cosmic-osd. The simplest path:

```bash
# Mark cosmic-osd binary unexecutable so cosmic-session's spawn fails:
sudo chmod -x /usr/bin/cosmic-osd
# Then log out / log back in.
# Restore later with: sudo chmod +x /usr/bin/cosmic-osd
```

(A cleaner cosmic-session config knob is being investigated for v0.3.x;
see the [Polkit Agent wiki page](https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki/Polkit-Agent).)

On Hyprland / Sway / Wayfire / wlroots compositors there's no
competing agent and Sentinel registers cleanly out of the box.

## Upgrade

```bash
yay -Syu sentinel              # AUR
sudo apt install ./sentinel_0.3.1-1_amd64.deb
sudo dnf install ./sentinel-0.3.1-1.x86_64.rpm
git pull && pkexec ./install.sh
```

Log out and log back in for the autostart entry to fire.
